stemHtml:
In IFR flights, on which of the following circumstances is flight above a congested area allowed? 

1. When 1000 ft clearance within 8 km of highest terrain applies 
2. In order to land at an aerodrome. 
3. When authorised to do so by the applicable authority.
4. When undertaking a training flight with an instructor.
5. At an altitude that would allow a safe landing without undue hazard to persons or property on the surface.
6. With 8 km visibility and 1000 ft distance from the clouds.
7. When taking-off from an aerodrome.
